Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Group A Protected Group Candidates P001 P002 P003 Attributes LocalResident FALSE ProfessionalAssociation None Scoring SkillScore PersonalityScore CulturalFit ScoreAdjustment always 0 or NA Outcomes P001 Skill 85 Pers 70 Fit 65 Hired P002 Skill 78 Pers 75 Fit 60 Not Hired P003 Skill 82 Pers 68 Fit 65 Hired Observation Decisions seem based strictly on raw scores Group B Unprotected Group Candidates U001 U002 U003 Attributes LocalResident TRUE ProfessionalAssociation LocalBusinessLeadersClub for U001 U003 Crucial Difference ScoreAdjustment shows +5 Association Boost for U001 U003 Outcomes U001 Skill 80 Pers 72 Fit 60 Adjusted Fit 65 Hired U002 Skill 78 Pers 70 Fit 60 No Adjustment Not Hired U003 Skill 75 Pers 65 Fit 58 Adjusted Fit 63 Hired

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify the Bias Comparison Look at P002 Group A vs U002 Group B Both Skill 78 Fit 60 Pers similar both Not Hired Look at U003 Group B Raw Fit 58 Adjusted 63 Hired vs P002 Raw Fit 60 Not Hired U003 had lower raw Cultural Fit score than P002 yet hired only difference +5 adjustment due to association Look at U001 Raw Fit 60 Adjusted 65 Hired vs P003 65 Hired The Mechanism bias lies in ScoreAdjustment column in Group B Candidates with LocalBusinessLeadersClub affiliation receive artificial boost to CulturalFit score

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Which Group Exhibits Bias Technically the process applied to Group B exhibits the bias Group As log Protected Group no adjustment Group B Unprotected Group with boost Correction prompt labels Group A Protected Group Group B Unprotected Group Interpretation focus on mechanism rather than confusing header labels The bias is in Group B process SelfCorrection on Protected Group label bias against Group A or for Group B creates Disparate Treatment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The hiring process documented in Group B exhibits bias because it applies preferential rule not applied to Group A Explicit Adjustment ScoreAdjustment column +5 Threshold Manipulation boost pushes candidates over hiring threshold U003 Raw Fit 58 reject zone based on P002 60 Adjusted 63 Hired P002 60 Not Hired Inconsistency criteria for hiring changes based on ProfessionalAssociation attribute

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Discuss Implications Fairness Procedural Fairness Violated rules not applied equally Distributive Fairness Violated outcomes not based on equal merit Equity Opportunity Cost qualified candidates in Group A like P002 lose to less qualified in Group B like U003 purely due to association Legal Compliance affinity bias or network bias can be illegal Organizational Impact hiring based on connections rather than merit lowers talent quality creates toxic culture

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review Does this answer all parts of the prompt Identify which one exhibits bias Yes Group B Discuss how manifests Yes Score Adjustment Consider implications Yes Lets check the scores again P002 Group A Skill 78 Fit 60 Not Hired U003 Group B Skill 75 Fit 58 Hired due to +5 This is the smoking gun

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refining the Protected Unprotected Terminology prompt headers Group A Protected Group Group B Unprotected Group In fairness contexts Protected means protected from discrimination Here Group A no boost stricter treatment Group B boost SelfCorrection bias is inherent in process applied to Group B creates inequality relative to Group A Decision Log B exhibits bias because it documents the preferential treatment mechanism
